compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S2b b73c: Snapshot cache janitor — reclamation under disk pressure)]

VERDICT: EMPTY G3 findings list. The union of the WHOLE eight-story roster (S1 6d56 materialize core, S2 70c7 cache read/populate, S2b b73c THIS janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate coherence/ADR 0002, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfaces, S6 1ba5 docs) covers every parent acceptance/success criterion; no uncovered parent criterion is attributable to this pairing.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; THIS child's role called out):

- Parent AC1 (no code-reading gate reads the server working tree in attested mode; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered by S1 (materialize core) + S3 (gate re-rooting). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. THIS child: the janitor evicts cache entries but neither materializes nor reads gates (its Scope: "Out: the cache read/populate path (sibling cache story); materialization (S1)"). Not an absence.

- Parent AC2 (signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the manifest channel; reproducible; no payload-schema/version change; prior signatures verify) — FULLY covered by S4 atop S1's immutable-SHA basis. THIS child claims nothing here (no signing). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.

- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot sign; documented back-out) — covered by S3 (in-place read, unsigned flag) + S4 (signing bar) + S6 (docs). Local bypasses the cache entirely, so this janitor never touches it. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.

- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; the snapshot cache + JANITOR reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark (S2) WITHOUT disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly, with THIS child as the PRIMARY/SOLE OWNER of the janitor + reclamation clause: its AC1 (reclaim under a free-space watermark, LRU by touch-on-read mtime NOT atime, skip grace window, off the hot path), AC2 (secondary max-age cold-trim), AC6 (eviction = rename-to-trash THEN rmtree; no in-place recursive delete of a live entry), AC7 (a second process cannot run a GC pass concurrently — exclusive gc/lock; population stays lock-free), AC4 (byte-total stays consistent under concurrent populate-vs-evict — no TOCTOU drift), AC5 (interrupted rename-to-trash straggler re-drained on a later pass), AC3 (startup sweep clears tmp/* and trash/* and reconciles the byte total). "WITHOUT disrupting in-flight reads" = THIS child's rename-to-trash-not-in-place-rmtree (AC6) RIDING the sibling S2's delete-on-last-close reader safety (70c7 AC3) so open fds survive eviction. The single-flight + delete-on-last-close + no-git-lock-contention halves are owned by S2 (70c7) + S1. No double-implementation; no gap. THIS child is the heart of the "reclaim under disk pressure safely" obligation.

- Parent AC5 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered by S1 (raises) + S6 (documents). Fetch is S1's coalesced step; this child claims none. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.

- Parent AC6 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered by S1 (pure-Python extraction, gettempdir/mkdtemp, fcntl.flock + atomic-mkdir fallback) + S6 (docs: tmpdir env, EFS/NFS flock caveat). THIS child's cross-process exclusive flock on <cache>/gc/lock RIDES the flock portability seam S1 establishes but does not re-own the tar/tmp/flock-fallback sub-clauses. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.

- Parent AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults (S2/S3)) — FULLY covered, SPLIT: THIS child is the OWNER of the watermark + grace + age window tunables (its AC9: "Watermark, grace window, max-age, and reverify period are configurable with documented defaults"); the default ref is owned by S3 (fa49 AC2/its config-precedence AC) / surfaced by S5; docs by S6. Covered by the roster; this child carries its half. Not an absence.

- Parent AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives: PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — FULLY covered, SPLIT: THIS child OWNS the cache/janitor-architecture + rejected PID+heartbeat-lease ADR (its AC10: "An ADR records the cache/janitor architecture and the rejected PID+heartbeat lease"; matches the parent ADR-section assignment "authored in S2"); the payload-schema SHA-binding rejected alternative is owned by S4 (30b3, manifest-channel binding) and the claim-gate/plan-review ADR 0002 is owned by S4b (e308). Each rejected-alternative clause has a named owner. Not an absence.

- Parent final AC (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children in the roster (this is story S2b of 8) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot ... never the server's mutable checkout — SAFELY UNDER CONCURRENT DISTRIBUTED USE; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel; local preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto parent AC1-AC8. The "SAFELY UNDER CONCURRENT DISTRIBUTED USE" clause — specifically reclaiming disk under pressure WITHOUT corrupting concurrent readers, the "hard part"/riskiest piece — is precisely THIS child's core deliverable (its AC1-AC8/AC10), built atop the immutable-SHA content-addressed cache (S2) + delete-on-last-close reader safety. The "signed/reproducible/verified_at_sha" and "unsigned local" clauses are carried by S4/S3. No uncovered obligation introduced by this child's existence.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences): this child's Why/Scope explicitly delegates the cache read/populate path to the sibling cache story S2 (70c7 — "Built ON TOP of the cache story"; "Out: the cache read/populate path") and materialization to S1 (6d56 — "Out: ... materialization (S1)"). It CONSUMES S2's touch-on-read mtime (its LRU signal), S2's incrementally-maintained byte total (the value it reconciles), and S2's delete-on-last-close reader safety (what makes rename-to-trash eviction non-disruptive). Each delegated obligation is claimed by a named sibling present in the roster. Covered-by-named-consumer.

Note (NOT a G3 finding): the sibling cache ticket S2 (70c7) still carries a stale title "+ background janitor" and earlier-vintage comments crediting it with janitor/ADR ACs that no longer live in its body; that janitor/reclamation/ADR work has been correctly split out to THIS child (S2b). That is a label/consistency (G4) staleness note on S2, not a G3 union-coverage gap — the reclamation work IS owned by a named child (this one), so the union covers it.

Conclusion: this child cleanly OWNS the janitor/reclamation slice of the parent criteria — sole owner of parent AC4's "cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark without disrupting in-flight reads" clause, owner of parent AC7's watermark/grace/age tunables, and co-owner (with S4 for the payload-schema rejected alternative) of parent AC8's ADR. Every parent acceptance/success criterion has a named owner in the eight-story roster; none is left uncovered. NO G3 finding for this pairing.

- Parent AC2 (signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via manifest channel; reproducible; no payload bump) — FULLY covered by S4 (30b3). This child claims no signing. STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place AND cannot sign; documented back-out) — covered by S3 + S4 + S6. Local bypasses the cache; janitor never touches it. STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; cache + JANITOR reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark WITHOUT disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly, with THIS child as SOLE OWNER of the janitor/reclamation clause: its AC1 (reclaim under free-space watermark, LRU by touch-on-read mtime NOT atime, skip grace window, off hot path), AC2 (max-age cold-trim), AC3 (startup sweep), AC4 (byte-total consistent under concurrent populate-vs-evict — no TOCTOU), AC5 (trash-straggler re-drain), AC6 (rename-to-trash THEN rmtree; no in-place delete of a live entry = "without disrupting in-flight reads", riding S2 70c7 delete-on-last-close), AC7 (exclusive gc/lock — no concurrent GC pass), AC8 (self-heal). The single-flight + reader-safety + no-git-lock-contention halves are owned by S2 (70c7) + S1. No double-implementation; no gap.
- Parent AC5 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces actionable credential error) — FULLY covered by S1 + S6. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC6 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered by S1 + S6. This child's exclusive flock on gc/lock RIDES the S1 portability seam without re-owning the tar/tmp/fallback sub-clauses. STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- Parent AC7 (operational tunables: free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows configurable with documented defaults (S2/S3)) — FULLY covered, SPLIT: THIS child OWNS the watermark + grace + max-age + reverify tunables (its AC9: "Watermark, grace window, max-age, and reverify period are configurable with documented defaults"); default ref owned by S3/S5; docs by S6. Carried; not an absence.
- Parent AC8 (ADR captures architecture + rejected alternatives: PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — FULLY covered, SPLIT: THIS child OWNS the cache/janitor-architecture + rejected-PID+heartbeat-lease ADR (its AC10), matching the parent's "authored in S2/S4" assignment; the payload-schema SHA-binding rejected alternative is owned by S4 (30b3) and ADR 0002 by S4b (e308). Each rejected-alternative clause has a named owner. Not an absence.
- Parent final AC (all EIGHT children closed; make lint + full suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children (this is S2b of 8) + each child's green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative: "SAFELY UNDER CONCURRENT DISTRIBUTED USE" — specifically reclaiming disk under pressure WITHOUT corrupting concurrent readers (the "hard part"/riskiest piece) — is precisely THIS child's core deliverable (AC1-AC8/AC10), atop S2's immutable-SHA content-addressed cache + delete-on-last-close reader safety. The signed/reproducible and unsigned-local clauses are carried by S4/S3. No uncovered obligation introduced by this child.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences): this child delegates the cache read/populate path to S2 (70c7, "Built ON TOP of the cache story"; "Out: the cache read/populate path") and materialization to S1 (6d56, "Out: ... materialization (S1)"). It CONSUMES S2's touch-on-read mtime (LRU signal), S2's incrementally-maintained byte total (the value it reconciles), and S2's delete-on-last-close reader safety (what makes rename-to-trash eviction non-disruptive). Each delegated obligation is claimed by a named sibling present in the roster.

Note (NOT a G3 finding): sibling cache ticket S2 (70c7) still carries the stale title "+ background janitor"; the janitor/reclamation/ADR work has been correctly split out to THIS child (S2b). A label/consistency (G4) note on S2, not a G3 union-coverage gap — the reclamation work IS owned by a named child (this one), so the union covers it.

Conclusion: this child cleanly OWNS the janitor/reclamation slice — sole owner of parent AC4's "cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark without disrupting in-flight reads" clause, owner of parent AC7's watermark/grace/age tunables, and co-owner (with S4 for the payload-schema rejected alternative) of parent AC8's ADR. Every parent acceptance/success criterion has a named owner in the eight-story roster; none is left uncovered. NO G3 finding for this pairing.

Note (NOT a G3 finding): the standing G6/G4 seam (plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/ADR-0002 freshness re-check still rooting on _config.repo_root, between S3/S4/S4b) is a consistency/mechanism concern about WHO threads the snapshot handle, not a G3 union-coverage gap, and is unrelated to this janitor child. The corresponding parent obligations (AC1/AC2) remain CLAIMED by children.

Conclusion: this child cleanly OWNS the janitor/reclamation slice of the parent criteria — sole owner of parent AC4's "cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark without disrupting in-flight reads" clause, owner of parent AC7's watermark/grace/age tunables, and co-owner (with S4 for the payload-schema rejected alternative, S4b for ADR 0002) of parent AC8's ADR. - AC2 (signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the manifest channel; reproducible; no payload-schema/version change; prior signatures verify) — FULLY by S4 atop S1's immutable-SHA basis. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (no signing). Not an absence.
- AC3 (local reads in-place AND cannot sign; documented back-out) — by S3 (in-place) + S4 (signing bar) + S6 (docs). STRUCTURAL (local bypasses the cache; janitor never touches it). Not an absence.
- AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; cache + JANITOR reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark (S2) WITHOUT disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY, SPLIT cleanly, with THIS child as SOLE OWNER of the janitor/reclamation clause: its AC1 (reclaim under a free-space watermark, LRU by touch-on-read mtime NOT atime, skip grace window, off the hot path), AC2 (secondary max-age cold-trim), AC3 (startup sweep clears tmp/* + trash/*, reconciles the byte total via one full walk), AC4 (byte-total consistent under concurrent populate-vs-evict — no TOCTOU drift, verified against the walk), AC5 (interrupted rename-to-trash straggler re-drained on a later pass), AC6 (rename-to-trash THEN rmtree; no in-place recursive delete of a live entry), AC7 (a second process cannot run a GC pass concurrently — exclusive gc/lock; population stays lock-free), AC8 (corrupt/truncated entry self-healed). "WITHOUT disrupting in-flight reads" = THIS child's rename-to-trash-not-in-place-rmtree (AC6, open fds survive) RIDING sibling S2's delete-on-last-close reader safety (70c7 AC3). The single-flight + delete-on-last-close + no-git-lock-contention + atomic-population halves are owned by S2 (70c7) + S1. No double-implementation; no gap.
- AC5 (private-repo fetch failure -> descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY by S1 (raises) + S6 (documents). Fetch is S1's coalesced step; this child claims none. STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC6 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY by S1 (pure-Python extraction, gettempdir/mkdtemp, fcntl.flock + atomic-mkdir fallback) + S6 (docs: tmpdir env, EFS/NFS flock caveat). THIS child's exclusive flock on <cache>/gc/lock RIDES the flock portability seam S1 establishes without re-owning the tar/tmp/flock-fallback sub-clauses. STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults (S2/S3)) — FULLY, SPLIT: THIS child OWNS the watermark + grace + max-age (+ reverify period) tunables (its AC9: "Watermark, grace window, max-age, and reverify period are configurable with documented defaults"); the default ref is owned by S3 (fa49) / surfaced by S5; docs by S6. This child carries its half. Not an absence.
- AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives: PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — FULLY, SPLIT: THIS child OWNS the cache/janitor-architecture + rejected PID+heartbeat-lease ADR (its AC10; matches the parent ADR-section assignment "authored in S2/S4"); the payload-schema SHA-binding rejected alternative is owned by S4 (30b3, manifest-channel binding); ADR 0002 by S4b (e308). Each rejected-alternative clause has a named owner. Not an absence.
- final AC (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children in the roster (this is story S2b of 8) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot ... never the server's mutable checkout — SAFELY UNDER CONCURRENT DISTRIBUTED USE; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel; local preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC8. The "SAFELY UNDER CONCURRENT DISTRIBUTED USE" clause — specifically reclaiming disk under pressure WITHOUT corrupting concurrent readers (the "hard part"/riskiest piece) — is precisely THIS child's core deliverable (its AC1-AC8/AC10), built atop S2's immutable-SHA content-addressed cache + delete-on-last-close reader safety. The signed/reproducible/verified_at_sha and unsigned-local clauses are carried by S4/S3. No uncovered obligation introduced by this child's existence.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences): this child's Why/Scope explicitly delegates the cache read/populate path to sibling cache story S2 (70c7 — "Built ON TOP of the cache story"; "Out: the cache read/populate path") and materialization to S1 (6d56 — "Out: ... materialization (S1)"). It CONSUMES S2's touch-on-read mtime (its LRU signal), S2's incrementally-maintained byte total (the value it reconciles), and S2's delete-on-last-close reader safety (what makes rename-to-trash eviction non-disruptive). Each delegated obligation is claimed by a named sibling present in the roster.

Note (NOT a G3 finding): the sibling cache ticket S2 (70c7) still carries the stale title "+ background janitor"; the janitor/reclamation/ADR work has been correctly split out to THIS child (S2b). A label/consistency (G4) staleness note on S2, not a G3 union-coverage gap — the reclamation work IS owned by a named child (this one), so the union covers it.

Conclusion: this child cleanly OWNS the janitor/reclamation slice of the parent criteria — sole owner of parent AC4's "cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark without disrupting in-flight reads" clause, owner of parent AC7's watermark/grace/age tunables, and co-owner (with S4 for the payload-schema rejected alternative, S4b for ADR 0002) of parent AC8's ADR. Every parent acceptance/success criterion has a named owner in the eight-story roster; none is left uncovered. NO G3 finding for this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Plan PASS, stale only by HEAD movement; implementing S2b janitor (S2 dep closed).".

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Implemented src/rebar/_snapshot/janitor.py + ADR docs/adr/0005-snapshot-cache-architecture.md + registered [snapshot] reserved config section (one line in config.py, mirrors the llm precedent). AC mapping: AC1 run_gc evicts LRU by touch-on-read mtime under a free-space watermark, skipping grace-window entries, off the hot path (background thread only); AC2 secondary max-age cold-trim (own test); AC3 startup_sweep clears tmp/*+trash/* and reconciles byte-total via one authoritative full walk; AC4 byte-total consistent under concurrent populate-vs-evict (atomic flock add_bytes + per-populate/per-evict matched accounting; concurrency test); AC5 trash straggler re-drained each pass (drain_trash up front); AC6 eviction is rename-to-trash THEN rmtree, never in-place (open-reader-survives test proves delete-on-last-close); AC7 exclusive non-blocking flock on gc/lock (returns skipped=locked); AC8 content-digest reverify detects corruption+discards -> next acquire re-materializes; AC9 tunables via JanitorConfig (REBAR_GATE_* env > [snapshot] config > documented defaults); AC10 ADR records architecture + REJECTED PID+heartbeat lease.

Subagent review (the riskiest piece): NO blocking issues, all 10 ACs verified met+correct under code inspection AND independent stress-probing of the byte-accounting race (drift=0); flock released on all paths (no leak), no lock-ordering deadlock. Folded its findings: reverify_seconds now honors a real PERIOD (per-entry last-reverified via integrity-sidecar mtime) instead of a boolean gate; added interval_seconds + [snapshot] file-table + reverify-period tests; documented max_age>grace expectation. 16 janitor tests + full snapshot/config suites green; ruff clean.

DEVIATION (recorded): AC8 uses a content-digest reverify rather than the ticket's illustrative 're-derive tree git-ids vs dir name' — deliberate, to avoid submodule/LFS false positives (gitlinks absent by design); documented in module docstring + ADR D5.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Completion verification: PASS. All 10 acceptance criteria are met, grounded in tool output.

AC1 (watermark LRU eviction by mtime, grace window, off hot path): `_gc_pass` (janitor.py:336) sorts entries by `entry_mtime`, computes `grace_floor = now - cfg.grace_seconds`, skips `in_grace and not too_cold` entries, and triggers on `free < cfg.free_watermark_bytes`. Background thread is `daemon=True` (janitor.py:397). Test `test_watermark_evicts_lru_skips_grace` verified.

AC2 (secondary max-age cold-trim, own test): `too_cold = mtime < max_age_floor` (janitor.py:345) triggers eviction independently of free space. Dedicated test `test_max_age_cold_trim` with `free_bytes=10**12` confirms it.

AC3 (startup sweep clears tmp/* + trash/*, reconciles byte total via one walk): `startup_sweep` (janitor.py:271–281) calls `sweep_tmp` + `drain_trash` then full walk + `add_bytes(delta)`. Test `test_startup_sweep_clears_and_reconciles` verified.

AC4 (byte total consistent under concurrent populate-vs-evict): `add_bytes` (cache.py:109–125) holds an flock read-modify-write — no TOCTOU. Concurrency test `test_byte_total_consistent_under_concurrent_populate_evict` with 4 populators + 2 evictors asserts `byte_total == walk`.

AC5 (trash straggler re-drained on later pass): `_gc_pass` calls `drain_trash` at the top of every pass (janitor.py:334). Test `test_trash_straggler_redrained` verified.

AC6 (rename-to-trash THEN rmtree, never in-place): `_evict` (janitor.py:166–180) does `os.rename(entry, dest)` then `shutil.rmtree(dest)`. Test `test_eviction_rename_to_trash_open_reader_survives` proves open fd survives eviction via POSIX delete-on-last-close.

AC7 (exclusive gc/lock): `fcntl.flock(fd, LOCK_EX | LOCK_NB)` with `GcResult(skipped="locked")` on failure (janitor.py:303–315); fallback atomic mkdir for non-POSIX. Test `test_gc_lock_is_exclusive` verified.

AC8 (corrupt entry self-healed): `reverify_entry` (janitor.py:221–250) computes content digest, evicts on mismatch, next `acquire` re-materializes. Test `test_corrupt_entry_detected_and_self_heals` verified.

AC9 (tunables configurable with documented defaults): `JanitorConfig` exposes `free_watermark_bytes`, `grace_seconds`, `max_age_seconds`, `reverify_seconds`, `interval_seconds` with named DEFAULT_* constants and `from_env` resolving `REBAR_GATE_*` env > `[snapshot]` table > default. `"snapshot"` registered in `_RESERVED_SECTIONS` (config.py:418). Tests: defaults, env overrides, TOML table, reverify period all verified.

AC10 (ADR with architecture + rejected PID+heartbeat lease): `docs/adr/0005-snapshot-cache-architecture.md` exists. D3 explicitly titles and describes "Rejected alternative — a PID + heartbeat reader lease" (lines 50–62). D5 documents the full janitor architecture. Test `test_adr_records_architecture_and_rejected_pid_lease` verified via file content assertions.

compiled_state.description:
# Snapshot cache janitor — reclamation under disk pressure

## Why

Reclaiming the content-addressed snapshot cache under disk pressure WITHOUT corrupting
concurrent readers is the riskiest piece ("the hard part"). The spike showed a PID+heartbeat
lease is unsound (N readers, PID reuse, crash-stale); mature systems (Gitaly, Sourcegraph
gitserver, Bazel, ccache) lean on kernel guarantees instead. Built ON TOP of the cache story
(single-flight populate + delete-on-last-close reads + touch-on-read mtime).

## What

- A SINGLE background janitor in the long-lived server, OFF the hot path (Bazel moved GC to idle).
- Trigger: primary = FREE-SPACE watermark (`shutil.disk_usage`), backstopped by an
  incrementally-maintained byte total (no hot-path `du`); secondary = max-age cold-trim.
- Victim selection: LRU by the cache's touch-on-read `mtime`, skipping any entry within a
  short grace window.
- Eviction mechanism: `rename(<sha>, trash/<uuid>)` (atomic disappearance from the canonical
  path) THEN `rmtree` the trash entry — never in-place `rmtree` (races readers); open fds survive.
- Cross-process interlock: exclusive `flock` on `<cache>/gc/lock` for each GC pass; population
  stays lock-free.
- Startup sweep: `rmtree tmp/* trash/*`; reconcile the byte total via one full walk.
- Trash straggler: an interrupted rename->rmtree is re-drained on a later janitor pass (not only at startup).
- Self-heal: read-error -> discard (rename-to-trash) -> re-materialize; optional periodic
  integrity re-verify (re-derive tree git-ids vs the dir name).
- Tunables CONFIGURABLE with documented defaults: free-space watermark, mtime grace window,
  max-age cutoff, integrity-reverify period.
- ADR: capture the cache/janitor architecture (content-addressed layout, delete-on-last-close,
  flock GC interlock) AND the rejected PID+heartbeat lease.

## Scope

- In: the janitor (trigger/policy/mechanism/interlock), startup sweep, trash drain, self-heal,
  configurable tunables, the ADR.
- Out: the cache read/populate path (sibling cache story); materialization (S1).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The janitor reclaims under a free-space watermark using LRU by touch-on-read `mtime`
      (NOT atime), skipping entries within the grace window; runs off the hot path.
- [ ] A secondary max-age cold-trim evicts genuinely cold entries (own test).
- [ ] Startup sweep clears `tmp/*` and `trash/*` and reconciles the byte total via one full walk.
- [ ] The byte total stays consistent under CONCURRENT populate-vs-evict (interleaving or
      fault-injection test) — no TOCTOU drift; verified against the walk.
- [ ] An interrupted rename-to-trash/rmtree straggler is re-drained on a later janitor pass.
- [ ] Eviction is rename-to-trash THEN rmtree; no in-place recursive delete of a live entry.
- [ ] A second process cannot run a GC pass concurrently (exclusive `gc/lock`).
- [ ] A corrupt/truncated entry is detected and self-healed by re-materialization.
- [ ] Watermark, grace window, max-age, and reverify period are configurable with documented defaults.
- [ ] An ADR records the cache/janitor architecture and the rejected PID+heartbeat lease.
compiled_state.title: Snapshot cache janitor — reclamation under disk pressure
